Implement Phase 9: Voice and TTS Integration
description: Complete the voice interaction loop: integrate Whisper STT (browser) and Piper TTS (Rust), implement IPC bridge for voice installation and status, and verify Push-to-Talk (Ctrl+Space) workflow.

title: TitleBar Refresh: Sleek integrated look
Phase 2: Visual Fidelity Alignment (Codex Look)
Implement project capability scanner design
description: Design the scanner/classifier for existing repos. It should detect AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, ai.md, .beads, .kspec, .mcp, .gitnexus, GSD files, RTK rules, provider config, hooks, and project health. Output should classify projects as fully initialized, partially initialized, third-party initialized, not initialized, or conflicting initialization.
notes: Created docs/project-capability-scanner-design.md. Design defines read-only scanner inputs/outputs, DetectedMarker and CapabilityScanResult contracts, required marker IDs, five initialization classification rules, warnings/blockers, upgrade proposal inputs, CLI/git health constraints, first implementation shape, migration path from current one-off checks, and implementation acceptance criteria.
close_reason: Defined the read-only project capability scanner design and verified the project still builds.

title: Define spec-driven product contract
description: Create the canonical app contract for spec-driven development: Project, SpecItem, AcceptanceCriterion, Trait, Task, ExecutionRun, Artifact, Provider, ContextSource, and capability flags. This should reconcile notes.md with the current Tauri/React implementation and decide whether Beads, KSpec, or an internal model is authoritative for each concept.
notes: Created docs/spec-driven-development-contract.md. The baseline contract defines the stable product entities, source-system boundaries, ownership decisions, canonical fields, and follow-on gates for scanner and spec-aware task UI. Key decision: Simple Code GUI owns the internal projection and execution/token/capability records; KSpec is preferred authority for spec items, ACs, traits, validation, task derivation; Beads remains task lifecycle backend; GSD is workflow adapter; RTK/GitNexus are optional capabilities; providers own transport/model metadata only.
close_reason: Defined the spec-driven product contract in docs/spec-driven-development-contract.md and verified the project still builds.

title: Implement Health Explainer UI
description: Transition from simple health percentages to granular diagnostic views showing specific root causes of project degradation.

title: Implement Automated Learning Loop Trigger
description: Add a Rust-side hook in gsd_engine/executor.rs that triggers a knowledge extraction (bd remember/Obsidian update) after a successful atomic_commit.

title: Finalize Agent Board Evolution: Task Cancellation UI
description: Implement the 'Cancel' button in the AgentBoard UI, bridge the IPC command to the frontend hooks, and verify real-time status updates.

title: Sync ProjectWizard with Unified Scanner States
notes: Objective: Update the ProjectInitializationWizard UI to leverage the new granular 'installed' and 'initialized' capability states. Improve the detection step to clearly distinguish between missing binary tools (e.g. beads not installed) and missing project configuration (e.g. beads not initialized). Surface the overall project health score in the UI.

title: Integrate Project Health into AI Runtime Routing
notes: Objective: Update resolve_routes in RuntimeManager to consider project_health_score when selecting models. High-risk projects (low health) should default to Tier 1 models to ensure initialization success.

title: Unifying Project Capability Scanners
description: Retrofit beads_check and kspec_check into project_scanner.rs, ensure health indicators are surfaced, and validate SLA performance.

title: Phase 15: Agent Board Evolution - Real-time Burn Rate
description: Connect the Agent Board's burn_rate display to the token_transactions data and ensure it updates dynamically during agent execution. Implement UI improvements for agent status and queue visualization.
close_reason: Completed Phase 15: Agent Board Evolution - Real-time Burn Rate. Integrated agent_id into token transactions, implemented server-side burn rate calculation, and updated the Agent Board UI to display costs per hour.

title: Phase 19: Token Accounting & Cost Observability
notes: Implement a precise token tracking system and a cost observability dashboard to give users transparency into AI expenditure and efficiency.

title: Phase 18: Agent Self-Correction & Reliability
description: Implement retry logic, exponential backoff, and self-correction heuristics for agent failures during AI runtime execution.
close_reason: Phase 18 completed: Implemented exponential backoff, circuit breaking, and reliability tracking in the AI runtime.

title: Implement feedback loop for model tier adjustment based on success rates
description: Update the routing engine to dynamically adjust model tier preference based on historical quality_score and task success rates recorded in agent telemetry.
close_reason: Implemented feedback loop in resolve_routes. FACTORED IN: agent quality_score for QualityFirst and Auto policies to prioritize higher-performing models.

title: Integrate agent queue size and burn rate into resolve_routes
description: Update the Rust routing engine to factor in real-time agent metrics when selecting providers, especially for LatencyFirst and Auto policies.
close_reason: Implemented telemetry-driven routing logic in resolve_routes. Factored in agent queue size and burn rate for LatencyFirst and Auto policies.

title: Phase 17: Telemetry-Driven Routing & Real-time Optimization
description: Integrate agent performance metrics (queue size, burn rate, latency) into the routing engine to enable load-balanced and performance-optimized model selection.
close_reason: Phase 17 complete: Integrated real-time agent telemetry (queue size, burn rate, quality score) into the multi-provider routing engine. resolve_routes is now fully performance-aware.

title: Phase 14: GSD Execution Layer & UI Fidelity
Phase 13: Frontend Integration
Implement PTY metadata tracking (title, path, pid) in Rust backend
Validate intelligence sidebar scan metrics rendering
description: Add or improve renderer-side validation for IntelligenceSidebar scan metrics. Ensure health score, file count, scan duration, and scannedAt rendering use ProjectCapabilityScan camelCase fields and do not regress when capabilityScan is null.
notes: Added renderer Vitest coverage for IntelligenceSidebar scan metrics. Test validates projectHealthScore, totalFileCount, scanDurationMs, and scannedAt are read from camelCase ProjectCapabilityScan fields by using snake_case decoy values. Also validates null capabilityScan renders Ready and omits scan-specific metrics/state. Validation: focused Vitest passed (1 file, 2 tests); build:frontend passed with existing large chunk warning; GitNexus impact for IntelligenceSidebar was LOW with no upstream dependents.
close_reason: Added IntelligenceSidebar scan metric rendering tests and verified focused test plus frontend build.

title: Audit project scan metric consumers
description: Audit ProjectCapabilityScan consumers and backends for the newly exposed scan metrics. Patch any mock, HTTP, or bridge path that constructs ProjectCapabilityScan without totalFileCount, scanDurationMs, or projectHealthScore so TypeScript and runtime contracts stay consistent.
notes: Audit complete. ProjectCapabilityScan renderer API type includes totalFileCount, scanDurationMs, and projectHealthScore. Production renderer consumers use camelCase metric fields only. Snake_case metric names appear only in Rust serde source fields and intentional test decoys. Tauri IPC/backend paths pass through the Rust camelCase serialization and no mock/HTTP/bridge constructor missing metrics was found. Validation: bun run build:frontend passed with only the existing large chunk warning.
close_reason: Audit completed; no code patch required. Frontend build passed.

title: Add scanner health metric tests
description: Add focused Rust coverage for project scanner metrics. Verify project_health_score is derived from per-capability health_score values, and that scan output includes total file count, duration, and health score without changing the serialized contract.
notes: Added focused Rust scanner tests for project health score derivation and scan metric serialization contract. Verified health score clamps/averages per-capability health_score values and serialized scan output keeps camelCase keys totalFileCount, scanDurationMs, projectHealthScore while omitting snake_case keys. Validation: cargo test project_scanner --lib passed (2 tests, existing unrelated warnings); git diff --check passed; GitNexus impact for calculate_project_health and scan_project was LOW; detect_changes reported LOW risk with no affected processes.
close_reason: Added scanner health metric tests and verified with focused Rust test plus GitNexus checks.

title: Finish project scan metrics in intelligence sidebar
description: Complete and validate the partially implemented project scan metrics/health score path. Ensure Rust scan output, TypeScript types, and IntelligenceSidebar rendering agree on field names and remain backward compatible where needed.
notes: Implemented additive project scan metrics completion: Rust ProjectCapabilityScan now includes project_health_score serialized as projectHealthScore, calculated from existing per-capability health_score values. Renderer type includes totalFileCount, scanDurationMs, projectHealthScore. IntelligenceSidebar displays health score and scan metrics using camelCase fields only. Validation: build passed; git diff --check passed; GitNexus detect_changes reported medium risk with one affected scan flow.
close_reason: Completed scan metrics/health score wiring and validated with build, whitespace check, and GitNexus change detection.

title: Migrate Persistence to SQLite
description: Implement sqlx in Rust backend and migrate JSON state to SQLite for better performance and history. Required for robust token tracking and task history.

title: Implement spec-aware task panel UI
description: Implement the spec-aware task surface from docs/spec-acceptance-task-surface-design.md in the React Beads/KSpec task panel. Requires GitNexus impact analysis before editing TaskDetailModal, BeadsTaskList, adapter types, and KSpec/Beads mappers. Preserve existing Beads/KSpec adapter behavior while adding linked spec item, acceptance criteria, traits, validation status, implementation status, derived tasks, and source-system metadata.

title: Design initialization and upgrade proposal flow
description: Design the new-project and existing-project upgrade flow from notes.md. The flow should show what files will be created or modified, compatibility notes, rollback plan, and presets for minimal, standard, full/spec-driven, guarded, and local-first workflows.
notes: Created docs/initialization-upgrade-flow-design.md. The design defines proposal-first new/existing project flows, presets (minimal, standard, full_spec_driven, guarded, local_first), proposal/operation/compatibility/rollback/verification models, file contracts for .simplecode/manifest.toml, project-profile.json, ai.md, and AGENTS.md, existing-file preservation policies, safe apply semantics, UI requirements, and follow-up implementation tasks.
close_reason: Defined the proposal-first initialization and upgrade flow design and verified the project still builds.

title: Add spec and acceptance criteria task surface
description: Evolve the existing Beads/KSpec task panel into a spec-aware surface. It should display linked spec item, acceptance criteria, trait tags, derived tasks, validation status, implementation status, and automation eligibility without losing current Beads/KSpec adapter behavior.
notes: Created docs/spec-acceptance-task-surface-design.md. The design defines the spec-aware task panel surface, data projection, Beads/KSpec mapping rules, visual semantics, empty/partial states, editing/source-of-truth rules, implementation sequence, impact-sensitive symbols, and acceptance criteria. GitNexus MCP/CLI was unavailable, so I did not edit React/Rust symbols; created dependent implementation issue simple-code-gui-8e7 for code changes once impact analysis can be run.
close_reason: Defined the spec-aware task surface contract and filed the dependent implementation issue for code changes requiring GitNexus impact analysis.

title: Prepare spec-driven development baseline
description: Compare notes.md and current implementation to prepare a spec-driven development baseline for the project.
notes: Prepared gap analysis and Beads backlog. Created follow-up issues: simple-code-gui-cqv (contract), simple-code-gui-g40 (scanner, depends on cqv), simple-code-gui-wux (spec/AC task surface, depends on cqv), simple-code-gui-dgl (init/upgrade flow, depends on g40). Build baseline passed with rtk bun run build; frontend warning only: index chunk >500 kB.
close_reason: Prepared spec-driven development baseline, recorded findings, created linked follow-up Beads issues, and verified build baseline.

title: Stabilize terminal initialization and refit logic
description: Address intermittent blank terminal states by making the isActive refit effect in Terminal.tsx more robust and ensuring fit() is called after terminalRef.current is assigned.
notes: Implemented dimension-first PTY model across all backend adapters and frontend hooks. Optimized terminalInit.ts to use pre-calculated geometry and reduced startup delay to 16ms. Zero-flicker terminal initialization achieved.

comments.author: Johan.P
comments.text: Completed backend API updates for spawnPty and implemented dimension estimation in useProjectHandlers/useWorkspaceLoader. Next: Refactor terminalInit.ts and centralize dimension calculations.
